heading: ## Falsifiability
body:
The following numeric thresholds define conditions under which the core hypothesis (χ_Ze(young) > χ_Ze(elderly)) would be considered falsified:
- If the mean χ_Ze difference between young and elderly groups is less than X (TBD) after controlling for age, sex, and recording site.
- If the 95% confidence interval for the difference includes zero at p < 0.001 (Bonferroni-corrected for multiple comparisons across frequency bands).
- If the effect size (Cohen's d) is below 0.5 in a preregistered replication with N = TBD per group.
- If the correlation between χ_Ze and chronological age is not negative (r < 0) with p < 0.001 in a sample of N = TBD.

heading: ## Pre-registration plan
body: The primary analysis plan will be preregistered on the Open Science Framework (OSF) prior to any data collection for the validation study. The preregistration ID is placeholder: osf.io/TBD. Planned preregistration date: 2026-12-01 (TBD). The preregistration will include: (a) primary hypothesis, (b) exclusion criteria, (c) analysis pipeline (including frequency band selection and χ_Ze calculation), (d) sample size justification, and (e) planned subgroup analyses.

heading: ## Sample size calculation
body:
The required sample size is estimated using the formula for a two-sample t-test (two-tailed, α = 0.001, power = 0.80):

n = (1.96 + 0.84)² · σ² / δ²

Where:
- σ² = pooled variance of χ_Ze (placeholder: TBD from pilot data)
- δ = minimum clinically meaningful difference in χ_Ze (placeholder: TBD)
- n = required sample size per group

Assuming equal allocation across three groups (young, middle-aged, elderly), total N = 3 × n. Current placeholder N = 161 (n ≈ 54 per group) pending pilot data.

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Low signal-to-noise ratio in wearable EEG | Medium | High | Use validated dry electrodes; apply ICA-based artifact removal; benchmark against wet-electrode data |
| Ze-theory not replicable across independent labs | Low | Critical | Pre-register analysis; share code and data; invite external replication |
| Olfactory sensor drift over time | High | Medium | Calibrate daily with reference gas; use baseline correction; replace sensors every 6 months |
| Participant dropout in longitudinal study | Medium | Medium | Over-recruit by 20%; use mixed-effects models to handle missing data |
| Confounding by medication or sleep | High | Medium | Collect medication and sleep logs; include as covariates in analysis |

heading: ## Limitations
body:
- The current validation datasets (N=4) are limited in size and demographic diversity; results may not generalize to broader populations.
- Ze-theory has not been independently replicated outside the originating lab.
- The olfactory module is at early design stage; no empirical data on sensor performance in wearable form factor.
- The 25–35 Hz band, while empirically optimal, may not be universally invariant across all recording hardware or montages.
- No longitudinal data yet to confirm χ_Ze as a biomarker of aging trajectory rather than cross-sectional age difference.
- The sample size calculation uses placeholder values for variance and effect size; actual power may differ.
